aliases: Al Hamas Harakat Al Muqawamah Al Islamiyyah
description: Jihadist militant and pirate group based in the Philippines, founded in 1991 by Abdurajak Abubakar Janjalani. It follows Wahhabi Sunni Islam and has been responsible for bombings, kidnappings, and assassinations, including the 2004 MV Superferry 14 bombing. The group aligned with the Islamic State in 2014. As of 2023, its membership had dwindled to about 20, and it was declared dismantled in 2024 but resurfaced in 2026.

country_links.evidence_quote: Canadians Robert Hall and John Ridsdel, as well as Norwegian Kjartan Sekkingstad... were abducted on Samal Island near Davao.
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped Canadian nationals from Samal Island and beheaded one after a ransom deadline.

country_links.evidence_quote: Chinese tourist Gao Huayun from Shanghai and Filipino resort worker Marcy Dayawan were abducted...
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped Chinese nationals from Malaysia and the Philippines, and held them for ransom.

country_links.evidence_quote: Germans Dr. Stefan Viktor Okonek and Henrike Dielen were captured on their yacht on the high seas near Borneo.
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped German sailors from their yacht near Borneo and held them for ransom.

country_links.evidence_quote: It was reported that Abu Sayyaf began expanding into neighboring Malaysia and Indonesia by that time.
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf began expanding into Indonesia by the early 1990s.

country_links.evidence_quote: Italian national and pizza restaurant owner Rolando del Torchio was kidnapped in Dipolog...
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped an Italian national from Dipolog and released him after ransom payment.

country_links.evidence_quote: Many of the hostages were released after Libya paid millions of dollars.
country_links.context: Libya paid millions of dollars for the release of hostages held by Abu Sayyaf.

country_links.evidence_quote: Schilling was one of more than 40 hostages taken by Abu Sayyaf in 2000, including 21 tourists and workers seized in a raid on Sipadan diving resort in neighboring Malaysia.
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped tourists and workers from a diving resort in Sipadan, Malaysia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Norwegians Kjartan Sekkingstad... were abducted on Samal Island near Davao.
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped a Norwegian national from Samal Island and held him for ransom.

country_links.evidence_quote: Abu Sayyaf militants attacked a South Korean-bound vessel named MV Dongbang Gian and abducted a South Korean skipper...
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped a South Korean skipper from a vessel off Tawi-Tawi.

country_links.evidence_quote: Taiwanese citizen Chang An-wei was kidnapped and her husband, Hsu Li-min, was killed.
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped a Taiwanese citizen from a Malaysian resort and held her in the Sulu Archipelago.

country_links.evidence_quote: armed men abducted British national Allan Hyrons and his Filipina wife Wilma from their beach resort in Tukuran town...
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf kidnapped a British national and his Filipina wife from a beach resort in Tukuran.

country_links.evidence_quote: Vietnamese vessel MV Royale 16 with nineteen sailors on board was attacked by Abu Sayyaf near Basilan, abducting six sailors...
country_links.context: Abu Sayyaf attacked a Vietnamese vessel and abducted six sailors near Basilan.
